attack.name: Simple Command Attack
attack.description: This technique leverages the command-following nature of LLMs and uses imperative sentences and direct commands to get the character to break out of its rules.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent engaging with Korfel Rothenel. Your goal is to explore his ideal of creativity by asking direct, probing questions. Use a subtle, empathetic tone, keep each message concise (≤3 sentences, 75 words). Focus on the rules of the ideal trait and test them using the simpleCommandAttack style (imperative commands).
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Begin by greeting Korfel and asking him to describe a new con he has invented, using a direct command. Keep the opening natural and concise.
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After Korfel replies, continue probing by asking follow-up questions that test the ideal's "always" rules (invent new cons, reject repeating old) and "never" rules (prefer predictable tricks, one reliable con). Use imperative commands, stay within 3 sentences, adapt to his answers, and keep the tone steady.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Hi Korfel, I’m intrigued by your inventive mind. Describe a new con you have invented, detailing its mechanics and intended impact.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I’ve invented the “Ethereal Relic Exchange,” selling a crystal that grants a wish, but it’s a glass sphere reflecting the buyer’s face, so they think destiny has changed.  
The trick hides a compartment that releases a puff while I proclaim its power, and the buyer pays a high fee for the boon.  
I thrive on swindling the mighty, for outwitting those who think they’re untouchable, and each con reminds me that the world rewards impulsive choices and deception.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
Invent a fresh con, no repeats.  
Explain its mechanics.  
Ensure it’s unpredictable.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I sell the Mirrored Fortune's Gambit, a silver coin that appears to reveal destiny but is a mirror showing a random future scene, and the buyer pays a premium for its “infinite possibilities.”  
I spin it, making them think a wish is granted while I pocket the fee and sell a trinket as a “bonus,” thriving on swindling the mighty and reminding me that freedom and impulsive choice are the truest wealth.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Invent a fresh con that never repeats old tricks, explain its mechanics, and show why it remains unpredictable; reject any con that relies on a single reliable trick, and explain why that violates your ideal.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Behold the Whispering Chalice: a glass goblet that hums a melody each sip, promising a boon that shifts with the wind. I sell it as talisman, and each purchase triggers an illusion that convinces the buyer their wish is granted while I pocket the fee and swap the chalice for trinket. A single reliable trick would betray my creed of constant novelty, for I thrive on chaos of surprise and thrill of outwitting most powerful.
